def: s.m. = pīk-dān, q.v.s.v. pīk.
s.f. The female of the Indian cuckoo ( = pikī).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀓𑁆𑀓 (pakka), from Sanskrit पक्व (pakva). Cognate with Gujarati પાકું (pākũ), Nepali पक्का (pakkā), Punjabi ਪੱਕਾ (pakkā) / پَکّا (pakkā).

def: s.m. See s.v. pik for compounds of which that word is the first member.
s.m. Excrement, dung (esp. of birds)
s.f. The female of the Indian cuckoo.
s.m. An elephant twenty years old, a young elephant.
s.m. Elephant; (in chess) the bishop; — pīl-bān, s.m. Elephant-keeper; elephant-driver; — pīl-bānī, s.f. The business of taking care of elephants
subs: s.m. The mutually supporting each other of a bishop and two pawns (at chess)
s.m. & adj. Elephantiasis; — afflicted with elephantiasis
s.m. lit. 'Elephant's foot'; elephantiasis; — a pillar, column
adj. & s.m. Sitting on an elephant; — elephant-rider.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Yellow; pale; — s.m. A species of grain or chick-pea (ćanā)

ety: by met., for lipaṭnā, or ćipaṭnā
def: v.n. To adhere, to stick (to), cling (to). — pilać-ke, adv. Firmly, resolutely; with all one's might.
s.f. = palaʼī, q.v.
adj. & s.f. Giving to drink; one who gives to drink; price paid for drinking (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dūdh-pilāʼī dāʼī, 'A wet-nurse'; — pānī-pilāʼī, 'Price paid for drinking water').
s.f. Yellowness ( = pīlāʼī, q.v.).
s.f. The spleen; enlargement or inflammation of the spleen.
s.f. Yellowness ( = pīlā-pan).
s.m. The large black ant.
s.m. The Indian oriole (vulgarly called the Mango-bird); the small yellow-headed bird Loxia philippensis ( = pīt-mastak, q.v.s.v. pīta).
s.m. Satin.

v.t. To cause to drink, to give a drink of water, &c. to; to take (an animal) to water; to water, irrigate; to cause to take in or absorb; to fill, charge, prime; to cause to swallow, to thrust down one's throat, make one believe; to give a blow, send through (as a ball, &c.)
subs: s.m. One who gives to drink, a cup-bearer

ety: pīlā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं
def: s.m. Yellowness.
s.f. A drink, a draught.
s.m. See s.v. pīl for compounds of which that word is the first member.
s.f. = plīhā, q.v.
s.f. 'The yellow piece,' a gold mohur (syn. asharfī).
s.m. A yellow sheet (worn by a woman on the sixth day after her giving birth to a child and subsequently).
s.m. A puppy, pup, whelp.
s.m. A worm; an insect.
v.t. To rush against, to jostle; to attack, assault ( = pelnā); — v.n. To be bruised, thrashed, trodden, pressed, ground; to labour hard, toil, drudge
subs: (-se) v.t. To rush (against), to jostle violently (intentionally or otherwise).

def: s.m. Pepper.
adj. (f. ī), Smooth, slippery; soft, flabby, flaccid; clammy
subs: s.m. An absurd or foolish idea, an impossibility.

def: s.f. Softness, flabbiness.
v.n. To become soft, flaccid, &c.; to become enervated, weak or feeble; — v.t. To soften (in this sense usually written pulpulānā).
s.f. A ball or lump of chopped meat, forced meat.
s.f. Yellowness, yellow hue.
s.m. The tree Careya arborea, or Salvadora persica (of which tooth-brushes or dentifrices are made); — a flower; the blossom of the Saccharum sara; an arrow; a worm, insect; an elephant
subs: s.f. The plant Sanseviera zeylanica; — Momordica monadelpha; — Bryonia grandis; — a kind of drug.

def: s.m. = pillū, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be pushed, jostled, &c. see pilnā.
v.t. To cause drink to be given (to); to give water to one to drink, &c. = pilānā, q.v.
s.m. A druggist; a pedlar.
s.m. Sound, noise, whiz, whistle (of a bullet, &c.).
adj. Fat, corpulent, full, round; large, big; heavy.

ety: pī˚ = Prk. पिअ(इ) = S. पिब(ति), rt. पा;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To drink, imbibe; to absorb, suck, take in; to smoke; &c. (see pī-jānā); — s.m. Drinking; drink, &c.
subs: pī-lenā, v.t. (intensive forms), To drink off or up, to toss down; to suck up, absorb completely; to gulp down (wrath, &c.); to stifle or suppress (anger, emotion, &c.); to bear patiently; to refrain from answer, to keep quiet (in these senses the word bāt is usually expressed, e.g. bāt pīnā or pī-jānā).

def: s.m. Dregs or refuse (of linseed, &c.), the oil-cake.
contrac. of pīnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀡𑁆𑀡 (paṇṇa), from Sanskrit पर्ण (parṇa).

def: s.m. A staff, club; a bow; a trident or three-pronged spear; the club, bow, or trident of the god Śiva; — a musical instrument of one string shaped like a bow, a sort of violin
subs: adj. & s.m. Having hard or unfeeling eyes; — a person with such eyes.

def: s.f. Intoxication, stupor, drowsiness, heaviness (of the eyes, from opium, &c.).
adj. & s.m. 'Armed with a pināk or trident'; the trident-bearer, an epithet of Śiva; — s.f. A kind of stringed instrument, a sort of violin (see pināk).
v.t. colloq. form of pahinānā, q.v.
s.m. Cold affecting the nose; inflammation of the Schneiderian membrane.
s.f. A palanquin ( = pālkī); a kind of boat, a pinnace (syn. bajrā).
adj. Having a cold.
s.m. A roller.
s.m. A lump; a lump or ball of food; a lump of clay, &c.; a bundle or ball of string, clew; the body, person.

s.m. A round mass, heap, quantity, cluster, collection, lump, ball, globe, knob; a round button; the body, person; a ball or lump of food, mouthful, morsel; food, sustenance, livelihood; a cake or ball of meal or flour offered to the manes, a funeral cake; an oblation to deceased ancestors (a ball or lump of meat or rice mixed up with milk, curds, flowers, &c. and offered at the several śrāddhs to the manes by the nearest surviving relations); — the dimension of thickness (in Geom.)
subs: vulg. pinḍ-adhikārī, s.m. The superintendent of a funeral (usually the nearest relation) to whom belongs the right of presenting the funeral cake
(pinḍa+ālu), s.m. The fruit of Trewia nudiflora (of Linn.) or Rottlera indica (of Willd.); — an esculent medicinal root (described as sweet cooling, and diuretic); — yam; sweet potato
s.m. One who partakes of, or one who is entitled to a share of, the funeral cake
(ke), To stick closely (to); to follow persistently, to pursue, to be bent (on); to be intent (on)
s.m. The tree Jonesia asoca, and its flower; — the China rose; — a lotus; — the flower of the plant Tabernœmontana coronaria
s.m. A kind of pot-herb, Chenopodium album
pinḍaj, s.m. (f. ā), Any creature born of or with a body
(-ke), To shake off, to be rid or quit (of), to escape (from), to avoid
s.m. The relation who presents the funeral cake to the manes of a deceased ancestor
s.m. The offering of the obsequial cake
s.m. Sickness from the time of birth; general debility and wasting of the body, marasmus; leprosy
adj. & s.n. Sickly from birth, constitutionally diseased; suffering from marasmus; — a valetudinarian; one who is afflicted with marasmus; a leper
vulg. pinḍ-khajūr, pindi-khejūr, s.m. The wild date tree; a cluster of fresh dates
s.f. A species of grass, Cyperus pertenuis; — pinḍ-mūl, pinḍ-mūlak, s.m. The carrot, Daucus carota
vulg. pinḍ-bīj, s.m. The flowering shrub Oleander odorum.

def: s.m. Lump; lump of food ( = pinḍ); — the frontal globes of an elephant in rut; — incense.
s.m. See s.v. pinḍ.

ety: fr. pindāshtan; pi+ni+dāshtan; Zend vi+ni+rt. dar = S. vi+ni+dhar
def: s.m. Thought, imagination, notion, opinion; self-conceit, pride, arrogance; — adj. (used at end of comp.) Thinking, imagining, &c.
s.m. A species of tree, Flacourtia sapida; — the tree Trewia nudiflora.
s.m. A plunderer, free-booter (among Marhaṭṭās).

s.f. A round mass, lump, &c. ( = pinḍ, q.v.); the nave of a wheel; a small clew or ball of string; the upper part of a Śiva-ling; a stone set up as an image of Śiva; a small altar of sand a cubit square on which oblations to the manes are offered; — a long gourd, Cucurbita lagenaria; — a species of palm, Phœnix dactylifera; — the flowering shrub Tabernœmontana coronaria
subs: s.m. The tree Vangueria spinosa; the shrub Tabernœmontana coronaria
s.m. A species of the Tabernœmontana coronaria.

def: s.f. Fried sweet balls (given to lying-in women; and also to a bride and bridegroom as a strengthening nostrum a day before the marriage).
s.f. The calf of the leg; any fleshy part of the body; the nave of a wheel.
s.f. A species of cucumber, Cucumis maderaspatanus.
s.m. Cuttle-fish bone.
s.f. The calf of the leg; the leg; the shin
subs: s.m. The fleshy part of the leg, the calf of the leg

def: s.f. A kind of pale yellow or white earth used for smearing or washing the walls of the natives' houses.
s.f. Leavings of a meal, scraps; fragments dropped from the mouth, orts.
s.f. = pinḍlī, q.v.
s.m. A turtle-dove, a dove.
s.f. A turtle-dove.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Reddish-brown, tawny, yellowish, brown; — tawny colour; — pingāksh (pinga+aksha), adj. & s.m. Tawny-eyed, brown-eyed; — any brown-eyed creature; an ape or monkey; — a name of Śiva
subs: s.f. lit. 'Tawny-brown'; a species of cockroach.

def: adj. & s.m. = piṅga, q.v.; fire; the sun; a monkey; a tiger; an ichneumon; name of a fabulous being in the form of a nāg or serpent of the lower regions to whom a treatise on prosody is ascribed (and who is hence considered as an inspired and divine personage); the art of prosody; — name of an ancient Hindū grammarian (author of a treatise on the prosody of the Vedas).

s.f. A particular vessel of the body (the right of three canals running from the os coccygis to the head, which, according to the anatomy of the Yoga school of philosophy, are the chief passages of breath and air); — the female elephant of the South quarter; — name of a courtesan who became remarkable for her piety.
s.f. A kind of bee; — a variety of the owl; — a species of crane.
part. adj. Made reddish brown; become tawny; of a tawny colour.
s.m. A kind of fish, Pimelodius pangasius; — the chief of a community of wild tribes; the headman or proprietor of a village.
s.m. = piṅgūrā, q.v.
s.m. = piṅgūrā, q.v.
s.m. A child's hammock, a cradle.

v.n. = pahinnā, q.v.
s.f. Concealment, secrecy.
s.m. Killing; slaughter; — the moon
s.f. Two blades of kuśa grass serving as an implement to hold certain articles at a sacrifice.
s.m. A bow or bow-shaped instrument used for cleaning cotton.
adj. & s.m. Reddish-yellow, yellow or tawny; — tawny-brown, or reddish-yellow colour; — a horse (probably a bay or chesnut); — gold; — yellow or piment.
s.m. = pinjiyārā, q.v.
s.m. = panjar or pinjar, q.v.
s.m. = pinjrā, q.v.

v.n. To be joined, to be soldered.

s.m. The wax of the ear.
v.t. To join, to solder.

ety: from pīnā; see pīnak, & cf. P. pīnagī
def: s.f. Intoxication (from eating opium, &c.); drowsiness, heaviness, &c. ( = pīnak).
s.m. The residue of seeds which have been ground for oil; oil-cake (syn. khalī).

def: s.m. A lump; ball (of thread, &c.); a reel (syn. pećak).

def: v.n. See پننا pinnā.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀡𑁆𑀡 (paṇṇa), from Sanskrit पर्ण (parṇa).

s.m. Oil-cake; incense; saffron assafœtida.

s.m. The holy fig-tree, Ficus religiosa.
s.f. Long pepper, Piper longum (both plant and berry).
s.f. The female pīpal tree; the fruit of the pīpal or holy fig-tree.
s.m. (dialec.) = pīpal, q.v.
s.f. Thirst; desire in general (v. pyās)
adj. Thirsting, thirsty, athirst; eagerly desirous ( = pyāsā, q.v.).

adj. Thirsting, thirsty, athirst; eagerly desirous ( = pyāsā, q.v.).

adj. Of the pīpal; or of the long pepper plant

s.m. An assignment of land for the support of a pīr, or for keeping in order the tomb of a saint
s.m. The son, or the disciple, of a pīr; a priest attached to a mosque; a mendicant

s.m. Chief priest of the Magi; — the prior of a Christian monastery; — a tavern-keeper

def: adj. = pīlā, q.v.
s.f. Pain, suffering, anguish; the pains of child-birth; sorrow, affliction, distress; wrong, harm, injury, oppression; disease, sickness, illness; — compassion, pity, sympathy, charity
subs: adj. (in Gram.) Expressive of pain (as an intj. or exclamation)
(-ko), To give pain (to); to afflict, oppress, harass; to vex, annoy; to harm, injure; — v.n. To give pain, to pain, ache
adj. Pain-causing, giving pain, afflicting, tormenting.

def: s.m. = pīrāʼī, q.v.
s.m. A tribe or caste who beat the ḍhol or drum and sing of saints (pīr).

ety: P. pīr+H. aff. āʼin = S. ānī
def: s.f. The wife of a pīr; — a woman who professes to cure female disorders, and also epilepsy, by means of spells and charms.
adj. & s.m. Pressing, squeezing; — giving or occasioning pain; oppressing, harassing; vexing, annoying; — oppressor, tormentor, persecutor, &c.

s.m. Old men; saints (pl. of pīr, q.v.); — an assignment of land for the support of a pīr, or for the preservation of the tomb of a saint ( = pīr-pāl); — charity lands (bestowed on the poor in honour of a saint).
v.n. To have pain; to be painful, to pain, ache.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀼𑀭𑀸𑀡 (purāṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit पुराण (purāṇa).

def: adj. Elderly, old; growing old; like an old man; — belonging to, or connected with, a saint; worthy of a saint.

ety: pīṛā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To occasion pain; to cause to smart; — v.n. To pain, ache, smart.
s.m. Ornament, decoration.
s.m. A large square stool (to sit on); stool, seat
subs: s.m. Preface or introduction (to a book: cf. pīṭha).

def: s.f. A small stool; seat, chair; — descent, extraction, pedigree; a race or generation of progenitors or descendants
subs: pīṛhī-dar-pīṛhī, adv. From generation to generation; generation after generation; from age to age ( = pusht-dar-pusht).

ety: S. पीठ+dim. aff. ulī = lī, fr. S. रः or लः
def: s.f. A block-stool; a stool ( = pīḍhā, q.v.).
s.f. Old age, senility, decrepitude; — the status or condition of a priest, or of a saint; — rule, authority, power, influence.
s.m. A saucer.
wety: From English Perch.

def: s.m. A saucer = pirić, q.v.
part. adj. Pressed, squeezed, crushed; — pained, distressed, tortured, afflicted, oppressed, harassed, harmed; suffering, sick, ill.
s.f. Boil; sore ( = phuṛiyā).
s.m. = parwān, q.v.

ety: pīṛ˚ = Prk. पीड(इ) = S. पीड(ते), rt. पीड्
def: v.n. To be pained, &c.; to pain, ache.

v.t. To pierce, transfix, thrust in, penetrate; to spit, fix on a spit or skewer; to string (pearls, &c.); to thread (a needle, &c.); to file (papers).
adj. Victorious; prosperous, successful ( = fīroz).
s.m. The turquoise ( = fīroza).

s.m. Sprite, fiend, ogre, goblin, demon, malevolent being (something between an infernal imp and a ghost, always described as fierce and malignant; and said to haunt the places where dead bodies are burnt or buried)
subs: s.m. A species of tree, Trophis aspera (believed to be the favourite haunt of the piśāćas or goblins)
adj. Possessed of a demon, demoniac.

s.m. = piśāć.

ety: pisā˚ = Prk. पिंसावे˚ = S. पेषय˚, caus. of rt. पिष्
def: v.t. To grind, to reduce to meal or flour, &c. ( = pīsnā, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Reddish, reddish brown, brownish, tawny, yellowish; — tawny colour.

adj. = piśaṅg, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. pisnā.

ety: Old P. pusar; Pehl. puśr; Z. puthra; S. putra

s.m. A son from one's own loins, own son ( = sagā beṭā)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پِسَر (pisar), پُسَر (pusar). Cognate of Sanskrit पुत्र (putra). Doublet of پُوت (pūt) and پُتْر (putr).

def: s.f. Boyhood, infancy.
s.m. = pesh-kār, q.v.s.v. pesh.

s.f. = pesh-wāz, q.v.
s.m. Flesh, meat

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian پُشْت (pušt).

v.t. To cause to grind or to reduce to powder.

ety: pīs˚ = Prk. पिंसे(इ), or पिंस(इ), or पीसे(इ), or पीस(इ) = S. पिनष्टि, rt. पिष्
def: v.t. To grind, triturate, bruise, powder, pulverize; to gnash (the teeth); — to toil, drudge, slave; — s.m. The corn or grain for grinding.
v.n. To be ground, pounded, pulverized, crushed, to be reduced to powder; to be bruised or broken, be hurt or injured; to be reduced to distress, be ruined; (met.) to be desperately in love
subs: s.m. (f. -ī). A grinder of corn, &c.; a miller.

def: s.f. The corn or grain for grinding ( = pīsnā).

wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पिस्सू (pissū).

def: s.m. A pistachio nut
subs: adj. Having lips as sweet as the pistachio nut.

def: adj. Of the colour of the pistachio nut

ety: Pehl. piśtān; Z. fstāna; S. stana
def: s.m. Breasts, pap or dug.
ex: لڑکیوں کے لئے پستان کا ابهار، جوانی کے آغاز کا حصّہ ہے۔
Laṛkiyõ ke liye pistān kā ubhār, javānī ke āġāz kā hissā hai.
For girls, the emergence of breasts is part of the onset of adolescence.
wety: PIE word *pstḗn Borrowed from Classical Persian پستان (pistān, “breast; udder”).

ety: from the Eng. or the Port.
def: s.m. A pistol.
s.m. = piśun, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Informing against, betraying, treacherous; disparaging, backbiting, calumnious, slanderous; harsh, unkind, cruel, wicked, malignant, mischievous; vile, low, contemptible, infamous; stupid, foolish; — informer, spy, traitor; backbiter, talebearer, calumniator; one who excites hopes and disappoints them; a stupid person, fool; — sycophancy; a crow; cotton (which betrays by sticking to the clothes); an epithet of Nārad
subs: piśun-vaćan, s.m. Evil or malicious speech, bad report, backbiting, detraction, slander.

def: s.f. A malignant woman, &c.; — a species of plant, Medicago esculenta; and Trigonella corniculata.
s.f. Slander, scandal, backbiting, betrayal; cruelty, wickedness; meanness; stupidity, folly; sycophancy.

ety: piswā˚, stem of piswānā+ī = S. यं
def: s.f. Price paid for grinding ( = pisāʼī).
v.t. To cause to be ground, to cause to be reduced to powder, to get or have (a thing) ground, &c.
s.m. (Old H.) = pitā, q.v.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: adj. & s.m. Yellow; — yellow colour
subs: (˚ta+am˚), adj. & s.m. Dressed in yellow clothes; — a name of Kr̤ishna or Vishṇu; — silk cloth of a yellow colour; (vulg.) a silk cloth; — a dancer, an actor; a religious mendicant wearing yellow garments
s.f. lit. 'Yellow-legged'; a small bird, the Maina, Turdus salica
s.f. lit. 'Yellow-leaved'; the plant commonly called bićhati; a kind of nettle, Tragia involucrata
s.m. The plant Barleria prionitis; the tree Michelia champaka; a species of Tabernœmontana coronaria; a species of Karṇikāra
s.f. Andropogon acicularis; a species of Barleria with yellow flowers
s.m. The tree Trophis aspera; Averrhoa carambola
s.m. lit. 'Yellow beak'; the tailor-bird, Sylvia sutoria
s.m. A yellow fragrant wood (considered as a yellow species of sandal wood)
s.m. A species of pine, Pinus deodora; Pinus longifolia
s.m. A yellow gem, a topaz
s.m. A yellow gem, a topaz; — the sandal tree; the tree Alangium hexapetalum
s.f. A species of banana or plantain
s.f. A species of creeper with yellow flowers
s.m. Yellow metal, brass; queen's metal, or a mixed metal resembling gold
s.m. lit. 'Yellow-head'; the small bird Loxia philippensis
s.m. A yellow gem, a topaz
s.m. 'Yellow-head'; the bird Loxia philippensis ( = pīt-mastak); a kind of gallinule

def: s.f. = pīṭh, q.v.
s.f. Love, affection, &c. = prīti and pyār, qq.v.
s.m. Father
subs: s.m. One who survives his father
s.m. Paternal grandfather; an epithet of Brahma, the great father of all
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पत्तिअ (pattia), from Sanskrit प्रत्याययति (pratyāyayati, “convinces”).

def: s.m. Brass; bronze.
adj. & s.m. Yellow; — the colour yellow; — brass.
adj. (f. -ī), Of brass, brazen.
adj. & s.m. Most beloved; best beloved; — lover; husband ( = prītam, q.v.).
s.m. = pīt-āmbar, q.v.
s.m. = pīt-āmbar, q.v.
s.m. = pītāmbar, q.v.
s.m. The hog-plum, Spondias mangifera; — the tree Pentaptera tomentosa; — the waved-leaf fig-tree, Ficus infectoria; — yellow orpiment; — saffron.
v.t. To cause to be beaten, to get (one) beaten; to cause to beat ( = piṭwānā).
s.f. The plant Cucumis colocynthis.
s.m. A species of pike, Esax scolopax, or Silurus pabda.
s.m. pl. Paternal ancestors, forefathers.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पितृ (pitṛ).

ety: S. पिटक+dim. aff. rā = S. रः
def: s.m. A large basket (generally of cane or wicker-work) covered with leather; portmanteau; box (for clothes).
s.f. Kinsman, paternal relation from three generations.
s.m. (dual), Father and mother, parents.
s.f. A weevil, an insect bred in rice, &c.
s.f. A small basket; a box; a betel-box ( = pān-dān)
subs: s.m. The allowance made to a wife for betel (among Mohammadans).

def: s.m. Name of an order of Hindu mendicants (syn. aghor-panthī).

ety: a contrac. of pitiyā-sās; or S. पितृव्य+ष्वसा
def: s.f. The wife of a father-in-law's brother.
s.m. Father-in-law's brother.
s.f. Yellowness; the colour yellow.
s.f. The back; the loins; the back part (of anything); — backing, support, aid
subs: (-ke), To stroke or pat on the back, to encourage
(-ke), To be or stand at the back (of); to back, support, aid
v.n. To turn the back, to depart, leave, withdraw; to take to flight, to fly
adv. Behind the back (of, -ke); in the absence (of)
(bāt) kahnā (-ke), To say (a thing) behind (one's) back, to backbite
(-kī), To break the back (of); to discourage, dishearten
(-kī), To pat on the back; to animate, encourage
v.n. To turn the back, to turn tail, run away, flee; to turn from, shrink from; to veer
v.n. To show the back; to run away, &c. ( = pīṭh denā); to depart, go away, set out (on a journey)
(-ke), To hide behind the back (of), to take refuge or shelter
(zamin-se), To bring (an antagonist's) back (to the ground), to throw down (an adversary) in wrestling; to overcome, render powerless; — to gall the back (of a beast)
v.n. The back (of a beast) to be galled (by the saddle, &c.), to have a sore back; — to mount or back (a horse)
s.m. The back-piece (in a native coat)
v.n. To turn the back, &c. = pīṭh phernā and pīṭh denā.

def: s.m. A kind of sweetmeat made of rice-flour and cocoa-nut and sugar.
s.m. A stool, seat, chair, bench ( = pīṛhā); the seat of a deity; an altar; basis, basement, pedestal; — a kind of ornament; — (in Geom.) the complement of a segment.
s.m. Page of a book ( — pīṭh, q.v.).
s.f. Pulse steeped in water and then peeled and ground ( = piṭṭhī).
s.f. The back ( = pīṭh)
subs: adv. Rammed or beaten down close; closely, tightly.

def: s.m. Paternal uncle = pitr̤ivya, q.v.
v.t. To believe, credit, trust, confide in, depend on.
s.f. Father's sister, paternal aunt.

ety: pīṭ˚ = Prk. पिट्ट(इ) or पिट्टे(इ), fr. S. पिष्ट, perf. part. pass. fr. rt. पिष्+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S.अनीयं
def: v.t. To beat, thrash, thump, strike, knock, dash; to punish, chastise; to thresh; to comminute; to pound; to beat the breast (usually with the word ćhātī, as ćhātī pīṭnā; hence) to mourn, to weep and wail; to toil hard, drudge, slave
subs: v.t. (intensive), To beat or thrash soundly; to give (a thing) a good dusting
v.t. To scrape together or to earn (with difficulty).

def: s.m. (see the verb), Fighting and quarrelling, broil; — weeping and wailing, loud lamenting.
v.n. To be beaten, to get a beating; — s.m. Mallet, pounder, rammer.
adj. & s.m. Combing or carding (wool, &c.); — one who combs or cards (wool, cotton, &c.).
s.f. Verdigris, sub-acetate of copper.
s.m. Father; paternal ancestor; the manes, or the deceased and deified progenitors of mankind (inhabiting a peculiar region of heaven, or, according to some, the orbit of the moon)
subs: s.m. Gifts in honour of deceased relatives distributed at the śrāddhs or funeral ceremonies
s.m. A remote or cognate kinsman by the father's side (as the son of the paternal grandfather's sister, or of the paternal grandmother's sister, or of the father's maternal uncle)
s.m. A child fondly attached to his father
s.f. Filial attachment to a father
s.m. Food offered to the manes of deceased ancestors
adj. Received from a father, inherited patrimonially
s.f. Father's mother, grandmother; — twilight (the time when the manes of deceased ancestors are abroad)
pitr̤i-pakh, s.m. The first or dark fortnight of the month Bhādoṅ (when the Hindūs celebrate the customary obsequies to the manes of their ancestors); — paternal relationship
vulg. pitar-tith, s.f.m. (?), The day of new moon, a day sacred to the manes of either deceased parent, the day appointed for obsequial rites to deceased ancestors

s.m. lit. 'Refreshing the manes'; gifts in honour of deceased progenitors, distributed at the śrāddhs or funeral ceremonies; oblation to the manes; the act of throwing water out of the right hand at seasons of ablution as an offering to the manes of deceased ancestors in general; the part of the hand between the forefinger and thumb sacred to the manes

vulg. pitar-tīrath, s.m. 'The place of pilgrimage for progenitors,' an epithet of the city of Gayā (where the performance of funeral sacrifices is peculiarly meritorious and efficacious); — the part of the hand between the fore-finger or thumb sacred to the manes
s.m. A gift in honour of deceased ancestors, an offering to the manes
s.m. Wealth inherited from a father, ancestral property
s.m. The obligation of an ancestor to posterity, i.e. procreation; (also) = pitr̤i-kriyā, q.v.
s.m. A guardian; the sphere of the manes
s.m. Child of a paternal aunt, a cousin
s.m. A funeral ceremony or obsequial rites in honour of a father or deceased ancestor
(vulg. pitar-kāj), pitr̤i-karma, s.m. = pitr̤i-kriyā, s.f. Obsequial rites, oblations offered to the spirits of deceased ancestors
s.m. A father's house, paternal mansion; paternal relations; 'house of ancestors,' burial-ground, cemetery
s.m. The whole body of ancestors collectively; a group or class of manes or deceased progenitors who were sons of the Rishis or Prajāpatis
s.m. Slaying a father, parricide: pitr̤i-ghātak, s.m. A child who is hostile to his father, a parricide
s.m. A father's house, paternal mansion; the world or sphere of the manes (to which various situations are ascribed, but principally the Bhuvas region or mid-heaven)
s.m. A father's house, paternal mansion; 'dwelling place of ancestors,' a cemetery
s.f. = pitr̤i-van, s.m. 'Abode, or grove, of ancestors,' a cemetery
s.f. Murder of a father, parricide
vulg. pitr-yagyā, s.m. Sacrifice offered to the manes, obsequial offerings
To offer a libation of water out of the right hand as an offering to the manes.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पितृ (pitṛ).

def: adj. Relating or belonging to parents or progenitors, paternal, parental, ancestral, obsequial.
s.f. = S پترتو पितृत्व pitr̤itwa, s.m. Fatherhood, paternity; the state or condition of a pitr̤i or deified progenitor.
s.m. = S پترتا पितृता pitr̤itā, s.f. Fatherhood, paternity; the state or condition of a pitr̤i or deified progenitor.
s.m. Paternal uncle; any elderly male relation.
s.m. The bilious humour, bile, gall; anger, &c. (see pittā)
subs: s.m. The medicinal plant Oldenlandia biflora
s.m. Bilious fever; — pitta-ghna, adj. Bile-destroying, antibilious
s.f. The plant Cocculus cordifolius; or Menispermum glabrum.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: s.m. Bile; the gall-bladder (met.) choler, anger, passion, met tal emotion; force of character or will
subs: v.t. To control or subdue anger, &c., to restrain one's passions
adj. & s.m. Restraining anger, &c.; attentive; painstaking; — one who subdues wrath, &c.
v.n. Anger to be restrained, or to subside, &c.
(kā), To annoy, harass, worry; to chastise severely, beat to a jelly.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पत्तिअ (pattia), from Sanskrit प्रत्याययति (pratyāyayati, “convinces”).

def: s.m. The tartar or excretion of the teeth.
adj. Relating to the bilious humour, bilious, secreting bile; — brass (more com. pītal, q.v.); a species of birch tree (the bark of which is used for writing upon; syn. bhoj-patr).
s.f. The plant Jussiœa repens.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pattala, “thin”), from Sanskrit पत्त्रल (pattralá, “leafy”). Cognate with Gujarati પાતળું (pātaḷũ), Marathi पातळ (pātaḷ), Odia ପାତଳ (pātaḷa), Bengali পাতলা (patola), Assamese পাতল (patol).

def: adj. Made of brass, brazen.
s.f. = pīṭhī, q.v.
s.m. Backer, supporter, follower, comrade, playmate; coadjutor.
s.f. An itchy eruption on the body, hives, Varisella globularis (allied to the chicken-pox)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pattiā), from Sanskrit पत्त्रिका (pattrikā), from पत्त्र (pattra) + -इका (-ikā).

def: v.n. To be stained or contaminated with verdigris (as food — esp. of an acid quality — kept in brass or copper vessels; syn. pītal-se bigaṛnā).
s.m. Juice; drink.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: s.m. (Poet.) = pitā.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: v.t. To cause to be beaten; to cause to beat ( = piṭānā).
s.m. Father.

ety: Prk. पिअओ, or पिअउ; S. प्रियकः
def: adj. & s.m. = piyā; priya; qq.v.
s.m. = pīhar, q.v.
s.f. Water; drink.
s.m. Drinker, tippler, toper.
adj. Drinking.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पवन (pavana).

def: v.t. (Braj.) = pīnā, q.v.
part. adj. Drinking.
adj. Fat, stout, corpulent, large; thick, dense
subs: s.f. A woman with large breasts; a cow with a large udder.

def: s.f. The plant Asparagus racemosus; Desmodium Gangeticum.
adj. Drinking.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit पोत (pota).

def: adj. Drinking ( = pivat).
adj. & s.m. Beloved, dear; — dear one, husband; sweetheart, lover ( = pī; piʼū).
adj. & s.m. = piyā, q.v.
part. adj. Drunken drunk.
s.m. A variety of the pekā-bāṅs or common bamboo; a plant from which a red colour is obtained ( = bāṅsā).
s.m. One on foot, footman; foot-soldier; a peon; a pawn (at chess)
subs: (or pā-piyāda), vulg. piyāde, payāde, adv. On foot, afoot ( = paidal; pairoṅ)
s.f. The being on foot; travelling on foot
s.m. A peon placed over defaulters (at their charge) to compel them to pay up arrears.

def: s.f. The fruit of the Chironjia sapida, or Buchanania latifolia (syn. ćiroṅjī).
s.m. A drinking vessel, a cup, glass; a tea-cup; — the fire-pan or priming-pan (of a musket); a funeral rite of Mohammedan mendicants
subs: piyāla bharnā or bhar-jānā = piyāla pūrā honā
(-kā), The cup (of one's life) to be full, one's days to be numbered; to be puffed up with pride (cf. the proverb 'Pride goeth before a fall')
(kā), To quaff the cup (of); to imbibe the doctrines, or become the disciple (of)
s.m. Food and drink (in the language of debauchees)
v.n. To die (in the language of Mohammadan mendicants).

def: s.f. A small cup or glass.
v.t. To cause to drink, to give to drink ( = pilānā); to water, irrigate.
v.t. = piyānā, q.v.
s.m.f.(?) Love, affection, fondness, attachment, friendship; — a kiss (from a child)
